title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
summary: Jacob E. Thomas argues that standard persona evaluation benchmarks fail to detect a failure mode called "Miranda distortion," where AI characters speak from cultural composites in training data rather than from primary documentary records. He proposes shifting from cognitive to epistemic simulation evaluation using domain expert raters, temporal grounding, and the pre-registered Prism Experiment to detect anachronism and documentary consistency instead of just fluency.
writeup: Jacob E. Thomas identifies a failure mode in AI persona evaluation called Miranda distortion, where simulated historical characters speak from cultural composites baked into training data (the musical Hamilton stands in for the historical record) rather than from primary documentary sources. The argument matters because standard persona benchmarks measure fluency and cognitive plausibility, which leaves anachronism and documentary inconsistency undetected. Thomas proposes a shift toward epistemic simulation evaluation: domain expert raters, temporal grounding, and the pre-registered Prism Experiment to check for anachronism and consistency with primary records.
